note: Seed sample IT knowledge base for a fictional client 'Northwind Clinic', so the app demonstrates the doc tree, search, wiki-links, frontmatter and backlinks on first run. All markdown; no real secrets anywhere.

docs.title: Core Firewall
docs.body:
---
type: firewall
hostname: nw-fw-01
client: Northwind Clinic
location: Server Room A
model: Fortinet FortiGate 60F
ip: 10.0.10.1
serial: FGT60F-XXXX
warranty_expires: 2027-03-01
criticality: high
source: manual
---

# Core Firewall

## Overview
Perimeter firewall and inter-VLAN router for the clinic. Single point of failure — if this is down, all sites lose internet and inter-VLAN routing.

## Specs
- **Model:** Fortinet FortiGate 60F
- **IP / VLAN:** 10.0.10.1 (mgmt VLAN 10)
- **Firmware:** FortiOS 7.4.x

## Access
> Credentials are NOT stored here. Admin login lives in the vault — see [[Firewall Admin Credential]].

## Dependencies
- Routes for: [[Network — Main Office]]
- Used by: [[App Server]]

## Runbooks
- [[Restore Internet After Outage]]

# App Server

## Overview
Hosts the practice-management application and departmental file shares. Backed up nightly.

## Specs
- **OS:** Windows Server 2022
- **IP:** 10.0.10.20 (VLAN 10)
- **Backup:** nightly image — see [[Backup & Recovery Runbook]]

## Access
> Credentials are NOT stored here. See [[Server Admin Credential]] (vault link).

## Dependencies
- Depends on: [[Core Firewall]]
- Documented in network: [[Network — Main Office]]

# Network — Main Office

## Topology
```mermaid
graph TD
  ISP --> Firewall --> CoreSwitch --> APs
```

## IP plan
| Subnet | VLAN | Purpose | Gateway |
|---|---|---|---|
| 10.0.10.0/24 | 10 | Servers | 10.0.10.1 |
| 10.0.20.0/24 | 20 | Staff | 10.0.20.1 |
| 10.0.30.0/24 | 30 | Guest WiFi | 10.0.30.1 |

## Devices
- Firewall: [[Core Firewall]]
- Core switch: nw-sw-01
- Access points: 4x Ubiquiti U6-Lite

## ISP / circuit
- Provider: Comcast Business
- Bandwidth: 300/300 Mbps
- Support line: 1-800-XXX-XXXX

docs.title: Restore Internet After Outage
docs.body:
---
type: runbook
client: Northwind Clinic
reviewed_on: 2026-04-02
owner: IT
---

# Restore Internet After Outage

## When to use this
Whole-clinic internet is down; staff cannot reach the internet or the practice app from other sites.

## Prerequisites
- Physical or VPN access to [[Core Firewall]]
- Admin login from the vault: [[Firewall Admin Credential]]

## Procedure
1. Check the ISP modem lights — if no sync, call Comcast (see [[Network — Main Office]]).
2. Log into [[Core Firewall]] and check the WAN interface status.
3. If WAN is down, reboot the modem, wait 3 minutes, re-check.
4. If WAN is up but no routing, verify VLAN interfaces and default route.

## Verification
Staff VLAN can browse; practice app reachable from a remote site.

## Rollback
N/A — read/restart only.

## Escalation
If WAN does not return in 30 min, escalate to the ISP with the circuit ID.

docs.title: Backup & Recovery Runbook
docs.body:
---
type: runbook
client: Northwind Clinic
reviewed_on: 2026-05-10
owner: IT
---

# Backup & Recovery Runbook

## When to use this
A server is lost or files must be restored.

## What is backed up
- [[App Server]] — nightly full image, 30-day retention, offsite copy.

## Restore a file
1. Open the backup console (credential in vault).
2. Browse to the latest recovery point for [[App Server]].
3. Mount and copy the requested path; verify with the requester.

## Restore a whole server
1. Provision replacement / VM.
2. Boot the recovery image; verify networking against [[Network — Main Office]].
3. Confirm the practice app starts and the firewall ([[Core Firewall]]) routes it.

## Verification
Quarterly test restore; record the date in `reviewed_on`.

# Firewall Admin Credential

> **Ledger stores ZERO secret material.** This page only points to the credential in your password manager.

- **Vault:** Bitwarden
- **Item:** `vault://bitwarden/nw-fw-01-admin`
- **Who can access:** Senior techs only
- **Rotation cadence:** every 90 days
- **Last rotated:** 2026-04-30

## What it unlocks
- [[Core Firewall]] admin console

## NEVER paste the password here.

docs.title: Access Control Policy
docs.body:
---
type: policy
client: Northwind Clinic
owner: Security Lead
reviewed_on: 2026-05-20
approver: vCISO
---

# Access Control Policy

> A governed space. This page has an **owner** and a **last-reviewed** date — the governance Obsidian has no concept of. Try the role picker (Admin / Reviewer / Viewer / Portal) to see who can edit, approve, or publish.

## Scope
Governs who may access [[App Server]] and [[Core Firewall]] and how credentials are issued.

## Rules
- Admin access to [[Core Firewall]] is senior-tech only; credential lives in the vault ([[Firewall Admin Credential]]).
- Reviews are required quarterly; an edit re-opens approval.

## Related
- Onboarding/offboarding: [[Staff Offboarding Policy]]

docs.title: Staff Offboarding Policy
docs.body:
---
type: policy
client: Northwind Clinic
owner: IT Manager
reviewed_on: 2025-09-01
---

# Staff Offboarding Policy

> This page's `reviewed_on` is over 90 days old, so Ledger flags it **needs review** — the kind of stale-doc governance auditors look for. Switch to the Reviewer role and approve it, or Admin and 'Mark reviewed today'.

## Steps
1. Disable accounts and revoke access per [[Access Control Policy]].
2. Rotate any shared credential the person held (see vault references in Runbooks).
3. Reclaim assets and update [[App Server]] access lists.
